NOAA HURDAT2 · AOML/HRD LANDFALL RECORD · 1851–2025

Every U.S. hurricane & tropical storm landfall, plotted.

681 individual landfalls across 174 years — including every stop for storms that hit multiple times, like Donna (1960) and Irma (2017) — sourced from NOAA's HURDAT2 best-track database. Hover any dot for wind, pressure, fatalities, and damage; click a dot to trace that storm's full track. Use the panel to filter by category, state, decade, or storm name.

18512025
On coordinates: landfall positions, wind, and pressure are the official NOAA HURDAT2 best-track values at each storm's strongest qualifying landfall — precise to the record, not approximated. Location labels are simplified to states (U.S.) or countries/territories (Full Atlantic Basin mode) using coastal boundary regions I built by hand from HURDAT2's lat/lon, since HURDAT2 itself doesn't carry that detail; small, closely-packed islands in the Lesser Antilles carry more uncertainty than isolated coastlines.

On damage/deaths: figures are contemporaneous (not inflation‑adjusted) unless noted, compiled from NOAA/NHC historical reports where a match was found; some HURDAT2-confirmed landfalls have no linked impact record and are shown as such rather than guessed.
Showing 681 of 681 landfalls
scroll to zoom · drag to pan
Sources: NOAA/AOML Hurricane Research Division — Atlantic Hurricane Database (HURDAT2), 1851–2025, for all landfall positions, wind, and pressure; NOAA NHC Tropical Cyclone Reports and NOAA NCEI Billion‑Dollar Weather & Climate Disasters for U.S. fatality/damage narratives where matched (impact data for landfalls outside the U.S. is not currently included). A landfall qualifies here if HURDAT2 flags it with a tropical-storm-strength or greater (34kt+) "L" record; multi-location hits show the strongest landfall's position. Built as a reference tool — always confirm figures against NHC's official reports for research or citation use.
